steps.title: Square both sides and rearrange
steps.content: Square $\sqrt{k - x} = 53 - x$ to clear the radical: $$k - x = (53 - x)^2 = 2809 - 106x + x^2.$$ Rearranging: $$x^2 - 105x + (2809 - k) = 0.$$

steps.title: Find the minimum value that gives exactly one solution
steps.content: Since $\sqrt{k - x}$ is nonnegative, any solution must satisfy $53 - x \geq 0$, or $x \leq 53$. The discriminant of the squared equation is $\Delta = 105^2 - 4(2809 - k) = 4k - 211$. For real solutions, $4k - 211 \geq 0$, so $4k \geq 211$. At the minimum possible value, $4k = 211$, the squared equation has the repeated root $x = 52.5$, which satisfies $x \leq 53$, so it is a valid solution of the original equation. Therefore 211 is correct.
